title: Recursively test for type piracy with Aqua

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation

title: Update number of ambiguities in tests

body:
## Summary

**Supersedes #5534** — same refactor, but renames the public terminology from "mass flux" / "volume flux" to "transport" throughout. If this PR is merged, #5534 should be closed.

Moves the model-agnostic open-boundary transport machinery out of `NonhydrostaticModels` into a shared `Models/boundary_transport.jl` and decouples its function signatures from `model.velocities` so they take the field tuple explicitly:

```julia
∮udA = open_boundary_inflow_transport(boundary_transport, velocities)
correct_left_boundary_transport!(u, u.boundary_conditions.west, Val(:west), A⁻¹_∮udA)
# … etc.
```

These shared helpers now serve:

- **Incompressible / volume-transport models** like `NonhydrostaticModel`, passing `(; u, v, w)`
- **Anelastic / mass-transport models** (e.g. atmosphere models in downstream packages), passing `(; ρu, ρv, ρw)`

Both satisfy the same solvability condition at open boundaries:

$$ \oint \rho u \cdot \mathrm{d} A = 0 $$

`enforce_net_zero_transport!` itself stays in `NonhydrostaticModels` — the zero-net-transport solvability condition it enforces is specific to `NonhydrostaticModel` (without a free surface). Downstream models compose their own variant from the shared building blocks.

## Why "transport"

"Transport" reads naturally for both the incompressible case (volume transport, units m³/s) and the anelastic case (mass transport, units kg/s), without privileging one interpretation. This keeps the shared API name accurate for both downstream uses.

## Renames vs. #5534

| Before (#5534)                              | After (this PR)                       |
| ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `model.boundary_mass_fluxes`                | `model.boundary_transport`            |
| `enforce_open_boundary_mass_conservation!`  | `enforce_net_zero_transport!`         |
| `initialize_boundary_mass_fluxes`           | `initialize_boundary_transport`       |
| `update_open_boundary_mass_fluxes!`         | `update_open_boundary_transport!`     |
| `open_boundary_mass_flux`                   | `open_boundary_transport`             |
| `open_boundary_mass_inflow`                 | `open_boundary_inflow_transport`      |
| `correct_{left,right}_boundary_mass_flux!`  | `correct_{left,right}_boundary_transport!` |
| `needs_mass_flux_correction`                | `needs_transport_correction`          |
| `{west,east,south,north,bottom,top}_mass_flux` | `{...}_transport`                  |
| `boundary_mass_fluxes.jl`                   | `boundary_transport.jl`               |

## Changes

- New shared file `src/Models/boundary_transport.jl` holds the model-agnostic helpers: `initialize_boundary_transport`, `update_open_boundary_transport!`, `open_boundary_transport`, `open_boundary_inflow_transport`, `correct_{left,right}_boundary_transport!`, and the area / per-side integrals.
- `enforce_net_zero_transport!` lives in `src/Models/NonhydrostaticModels/enforce_net_zero_transport.jl`, composed from the shared helpers above. It is private to `NonhydrostaticModels`.
- Old `src/Models/NonhydrostaticModels/boundary_mass_fluxes.jl` is removed.
- `NonhydrostaticModel.compute_pressure_correction!` calls `enforce_net_zero_transport!(model.velocities, model.boundary_transport)` (passing `model.velocities` explicitly instead of `model`).
- Shared helpers and `enforce_net_zero_transport!` are **not** exported — downstream modules import them explicitly with `using ..Models: …` / `Oceananigans.Models.…`.

No user-facing behavior change for `NonhydrostaticModel`. The shape of `model.boundary_transport` (a `NamedTuple`) matches the previous `model.boundary_mass_fluxes`, with field-name suffixes renamed accordingly (`west_mass_flux` → `west_transport`, etc.).

## Addressing review comments from #5534

- `enforce_*` stays in `NonhydrostaticModels` (tomchor's review comment that the zero-net-flux condition is specific to `NonhydrostaticModel` without a free surface).
- Shared helpers are not exported from `Models` (tomchor's "no need to export" review comment).
- Docstring wording: "Correct boundary values in `velocities` so that the combined net transport through all `OpenBoundaryCondition` boundaries with a radiation scheme vanishes — the solvability condition…" (from tomchor's suggested edit).
- Trailing comma in `Models.jl` export block (from tomchor's suggestion).

## Test plan

- [x] Package loads; symbol relocations resolve at include time
- [x] `NonhydrostaticModel` with `PerturbationAdvection` open west/east BCs: net `∫∇·u ≈ 0` to machine precision after 1 s simulation time (existing `test_open_boundary_condition_mass_conservation` — covered by passing `cpu-time-stepping-2-tests` and `gpu-time-stepping-2-tests`)
- [x] Steady state preserved for all three orientations (x, y, z) under `PerturbationAdvection` (existing `test_perturbation_advection_open_boundary_conditions` — same jobs)
- [x] Checkpointer round-trips `boundary_transport` (existing `test_open_boundary_condition_scheme_checkpointing` — covered by passing `cpu-simulation-tests` and `gpu-simulation-tests`)
- [ ] Full CI on GitHub — 70 jobs pass, 1 pending (`oceananigans-dot-jl` Julia ecosystem nightly); 4 jobs fail (`buildkite/oceananigans-distributed/pr/gpu-distributed-{hydrostatic-model,hydrostatic-regression,solvers}-tests`, all with `exit status -1`). Their CPU counterparts pass and the failing jobs don't exercise `NonhydrostaticModels` open-boundary code — likely an unrelated GPU-distributed runner issue, but worth a rerun before merging.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Share open-boundary transport machinery across models
